snippets.title: Express scatter with color and hover_data (recommended EDA start)
snippets.code:
import plotly.express as px
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
import pandas as pd

X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True, as_frame=True)
df = X.assign(species=y.astype(str))

fig = px.scatter(
    df, x="sepal length (cm)", y="sepal width (cm)", color="species",
    hover_data=["petal length (cm)", "petal width (cm)"],
    title="Iris — interactive feature scatter",
)
fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=9, opacity=0.75))
fig.show()
snippets.explanation: Express maps DataFrame columns to x, y, color, and extra hover fields in one call. update_traces tunes marker size and opacity for overlap.

snippets.title: Graph Objects training curves with unified hover
snippets.code:
import plotly.graph_objects as go

epochs = list(range(1, len(train_loss) + 1))
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=epochs, y=train_loss, name="Train", mode="lines+markers"))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=epochs, y=val_loss, name="Val", mode="lines+markers"))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Training curve",
    xaxis_title="Epoch",
    yaxis_title="Loss",
    hovermode="x unified",
)
fig.show()
snippets.explanation: GO builds explicit traces—ideal when lists come from a training loop rather than a tidy DataFrame. hovermode='x unified' shows all series at the same epoch.

snippets.title: make_subplots for loss and accuracy panels
snippets.code:
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import plotly.graph_objects as go

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, shared_xaxes=True,
    subplot_titles=("Loss", "Accuracy"),
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=epochs, y=train_loss, name="Train loss"), row=1, col=1)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=epochs, y=val_loss, name="Val loss"), row=1, col=1)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=epochs, y=train_acc, name="Train acc"), row=2, col=1)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=epochs, y=val_acc, name="Val acc"), row=2, col=1)
fig.update_layout(height=650, hovermode="x unified")
fig.show()
snippets.explanation: Shared x-axes keep epoch alignment across metrics. row/col arguments place each trace in the correct panel.

snippets.title: Custom hovertemplate with customdata (GO scatter)
snippets.code:
import plotly.graph_objects as go fig = go.Figure(go.Scatter( x=x, y=y, mode="markers", customdata=list(zip(sample_ids, y_true, y_pred)), hovertemplate=( "id=%{customdata[0]}
" "true=%{customdata[1]}
" "pred=%{customdata[2]}
" "x=%{x:.2f}, y=%{y:.2f}" ), )) fig.update_layout(title="Validation embeddings — hover for labels") fig.show()
snippets.explanation: customdata carries parallel metadata arrays; hovertemplate formats them for misclassification review on coordinate plots.

snippets.title: Save interactive HTML for sharing
snippets.code:
import plotly.express as px

fig = px.line(df, x="epoch", y="f1", color="run_id", title="F1 by experiment")
fig.write_html("experiments_f1.html", include_plotlyjs="cdn")
# fig.show()  # optional in notebook; HTML works offline with CDN script
snippets.explanation: write_html produces a standalone file teammates can open in a browser. include_plotlyjs='cdn' keeps file size smaller than embedding the full library.
